1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 079 — **three substantive prover lanes**, all targeting real sorry-count reduction).
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the path: Phase A (cohomology) + Phase B (differentials) + Phase C (Picard scheme, multi-step gap-fill) + Phase D (genus / Jacobian / instances, closed-by-reduction since iter-073) + Phase E (Abel–Jacobi, closed iter-073).
18	
19	Iter-078 was the second substantive-progress iteration in a row (net 17 → 15 sorries; -2). Highlights:
20	- `cotangentExactSeqAlpha.d_app` fully closed via τ-bridge `τ := adj_g.homEquiv.symm (adj_fg.unit.app S.presheaf)` — both alpha and beta now fully closed (single residual sorry in `cotangentExactSeq_structure` is the next Phase B target).
21	- `Modules.tensorObj` closed via direct sheafification of presheaf tensor product (sheafification of `F M ⊗ F N` with `letI` exposing the syntactic `R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` form).
22	- `h_diff_pi_smul_f` structurally prepared (`set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at the theorem head + `funext j` committed); substantive S2–S8 deferred to iter-079 Lane 2.
23	
24	Current project state (independently verified this plan pass via `sorry_analyzer.py` + `lean_diagnostic_messages`):
25	
26	| File | Sorries | Compiles? | Phase | Iter-079 plan |
27	|---|---|---|---|---|
28	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | 6 | yes | A | **Lane 2: close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (S2–S8 on top of iter-078's `funext j`)** |
29	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | 5 | yes | B | **Lane 1: close `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (re-enable preserved 7-step chain + 2 semantic gap-fills)** |
30	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` | 2 | yes | C0 | **Lane 3: supply `W.IsMonoidal` + route both instances through `LocalizedMonoidal`** |
31	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | 1 | yes | D done | off-limits (`nonempty_jacobianWitness` deferred to iter-085+) |
32	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | yes | C-rep | off-limits |
33	| `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` | 0 | yes | E done | off-limits |
34	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` | 0 | yes | D done | off-limits |
35	| **Total syntactic** | **15** | — | | |
36	
37	## Current Objectives
38	
39	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.
40	
41	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` — close `cotangentExactSeq_structure`
42	
43	**File status.** 5 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L122 `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf`, **L458 `cotangentExactSeq_structure`** (this lane's target), L742 `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega`, L759 `cotangent_at_section`, L901 `serre_duality_genus`.
44	
45	**Goal.** Close the single absorbed sorry inside `cotangentExactSeq_structure` at L458. The lemma asserts the three properties bundled together: $\beta \circ \alpha = 0$ (`h_zero`), exactness at the middle term (`h_exact`), and $\beta$ epimorphism (`h_epi`).
46	
47	**Why this is now reachable.** Iter-077 fully closed `cotangentExactSeqBeta` (via η-bridge). Iter-078 fully closed `cotangentExactSeqAlpha.d_app` (via τ-bridge). With both upstream defs closed and a preserved `/- ITER-076 disabled chain -/` block of ~220 LOC sitting in the file immediately above L458 carrying Steps 1–7 of the `h_zero` proof (named hypotheses `hα_fac`, `hβ_fac`, `hd_app`, `hcoh`), the closure becomes mechanical.
48	
49	**Mathematical content.** `h_zero` is `β ∘ α = 0`, proved pointwise: read off via `Universal.fac` on `α` then on `β`, with `hd_app` killing the derivation on the φ-image and `hcoh` resolving the adjunction-coherence equality. `h_exact` is exactness at $\Omega_{X/S}$ in the middle, established sheaf-pointwise: at each stalk the sequence is the standard Mathlib cotangent exact sequence `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`, which extends to sheaves via the stalkwise criterion. `h_epi` is $\beta$ epimorphism, established by the presheaf-level epi (via `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective`) extending to the sheaf via `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective` + an epi-from-presheaf bridge.
50	
51	**Recipe.**
52	
53	1. Re-enable the preserved `/- ITER-076 disabled chain -/` block above L458. It introduces named `set` bindings for `φ_g'`, `φ_fg'`, `φ_2'`, `adj_f`, the `hcoh` adjunction coherence, and the `hd_app` lemma. Verify that with both alpha and beta now closed, the chain elaborates without the iter-076 cascade errors that forced its rollback.
54	2. Use the chain plus `SheafOfModules.hom_ext` + `(isUniversal' φ_g').postcomp_injective` + the pointwise `ext U b` strategy to close `h_zero`.
55	3. **Semantic gap-fill #1**: introduce `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` as a single project-local helper above the `cotangentExactSeq_structure` declaration (or earlier in the file, after imports). The statement is roughly: a short complex of sheaves of modules is exact iff it is exact at every stalk; this transfers a (cosimplicial) stalkwise exactness check to the sheaf side. Apply pointwise to the ring-level `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` exactness already in Mathlib to close `h_exact`. Flag with `-- GAP-FILL:` comment.
56	4. **Semantic gap-fill #2**: introduce `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf` as a single project-local helper. The statement: an epimorphism of presheaves of modules whose presheaves are sheaves is an epimorphism of sheaves of modules. Combine with `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective` and `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` to close `h_epi`. Flag with `-- GAP-FILL:` comment.
57	5. Verify the file compiles and `cotangentExactSeq_structure` sorry count drops from 1 → 0.
58	
59	**Hard constraints**:
60	- **No additional project-local helper lemmas EXCEPT the two semantic gap-fills explicitly allowed**: `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` and `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf`. Each is a single Mathlib-shape declaration about sheaves of modules (not "thin helper chains"), flagged with `-- GAP-FILL:` comments. Per user policy 2026-05-11 these are permitted as semantic gap-fills.
61	- **No new axioms.**
62	- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation** of candidate bodies. `lean_diagnostic_messages` after each save IS allowed (standard dev feedback loop).
63	- Preserve `set_option maxHeartbeats 16000000 in` if present.
64	- Sorry-count budget: starts at 5; target **≤ 4** (close `cotangentExactSeq_structure`); hard cap **5** (no regression).
65	
66	**Blueprint**: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex` § "Cotangent exact sequence" — Lemma `lem:cotangent_exact_structure` and Theorem `thm:cotangent_exact_sequence`.
67	
68	### 2. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`
69	
70	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L502, L826, L854 (3 dead-end substeps — stay), **L1110 `h_diff_pi_smul_f`** (this lane's target, structurally prepared iter-078), L1150 `g_R.map_smul'`, L1179 `h_loc_exact`.
71	
72	**Goal.** Close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1110), the f-side per-summand R-linearity decomposition through the 5-layer functor stack, by completing S2–S8 on top of iter-078's committed `funext j` prefix.
73	
74	**State to build on (DO NOT modify).** Two iter-078 commits are essential prerequisites and must be preserved:
75	- `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at L411 above the docstring of `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`. Required because *any* tactic step inside the `have h_diff_pi_smul_f` block triggers `HSMul R (Z_i j) ?_` synthesis whose whnf cost exceeds 200000 at the theorem head.
76	- `intro r y; funext j; sorry` at L1093–1110. The `funext j` cleanly reduces the goal to the componentwise form `e₂ ((scK₀.f).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) j = (r • e₂ ((scK₀.f).hom (e₁.symm y))) j`.
77	
78	**Mathematical content.** The statement `h_diff_pi_smul_f` packages: for `r : R` and `y : ∀ i, Z₁ i`, the f-side cochain differential `scK₀.f` applied to the smul `r • y` equals `r • (scK₀.f y)`, computed via the canonical `e_i : ↑(∏ᶜ Z_i) ≃ scK₀.X_i` linear equivalences.
79	
80	**Recipe.** Build the substantive S2–S8 chain on top of the post-`funext j` goal:
81	
82	- **S2 (5-layer dsimp).** Unfold `scK₀.f` through `scK₀ → K₀ → cechCochain → cechComplexFunctor → toModuleKSheaf → toModuleKPresheaf_obj` to expose `(scK₀.f).hom = objD X (prev n)` where `X := (evalOp.obj P) ∘ (mk _ cech).rightOp`, `P := (sheafToPresheaf _).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)`. The `dsimp only [scK₀, K₀, cechCochain, cechComplexFunctor, toModuleKSheaf, toModuleKPresheaf_obj]` form is the iter-073 recipe; iter-078 ruled out the `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]`-first approach (smul instance fails to unify without first exposing the builder via the dsimp).
83	- **S3 (objD identification).** Use `AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD` to expose the differential as `∑ k : Fin (prev n + 2), (-1)^k • X.δ k` where `X.δ k = X.map (op (SimplexCategory.δ k))`. Use `CochainComplex.of_d_eq_succ` or `(up ℕ).Rel` case-split to handle the `K₀.d (prev n) n` opacity (the iter-076 surface).
84	- **S4 (`Pi.smul_apply`).** After S2–S3 exposes the indexed-sum form, apply `Pi.smul_apply` on the LHS to evaluate `(r • y) (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom)`.
85	- **S5 (`Finset.smul_sum`).** Distribute `r •` across the alternating sum.
86	- **S6 (`Finset.sum_congr rfl fun k _ => ?_`).** Reduce to per-summand R-linearity at fixed index `k`.
87	- **S7 (per-summand).** For each `k`, the j-component of `X.δ k` (read off via `evalOp_obj_map` + `Pi.lift`) is `Pi.π Z₁ (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom) ≫ (P.map (φ_k j).op)` where `φ_k j` is a morphism in `Scheme.Opens` with `V_j ≤ V_{j ∘ δ_k}`. Per-component R-linearity then follows from:
88	   ```
89	   (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_k}).op).hom ((presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_k} ≤ U).op).hom r * x)
90	     = (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r * (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_k}).op).hom x
91	   ```
92	   (RingHom distributivity `RingHom.map_mul` + presheaf-functoriality `← presheaf.map_comp` on the `(V_j ≤ U)` chain).
93	- **S8.** Close by `rfl` or `ring`.
94	
95	**Approaches ruled out iter-078** (do NOT re-attempt):
96	- `rw [Pi.smul_apply]` immediately after `funext j`: fails with "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern" — the smul instance on the RHS doesn't unify with `Pi.instSMul` without first exposing the builder via S2's `dsimp`.
97	- `show e₂ (…) j = r • (e₂ (…)) j`: fails with "failed to synthesize instance of type class HSMul ↑R ↑(Z₂ j) ?_". There's no standalone R-action on `Z₂ j` — only on the product, via `h_mod_pi₂`.
98	- `exact (LinearEquiv.map_smul e₂ r _)`: fails — `e₂` is `k`-linear, not `R`-linear.
99	- Constructing `L : (∀ i, Z₁ i) →ₗ[R] (∀ i, Z₂ i)` directly: doesn't avoid the work — `L.map_smul` still requires the per-summand chain.
100	
101	**Fallback** if S2's `dsimp` doesn't fire cleanly: `change`-based manual exposure of the smul (state the per-component smul formula via the per-i restriction map). The Mathlib lemma `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι` (`@[elementwise]`) provides the applied form.
102	
103	**Mathlib references confirmed iter-078**:
104	- `CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproduct.evalOp_obj_map` (in `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/Basic.lean` L383).
105	- `AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD`.
106	- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι` (`@[elementwise]`).
107	- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype` (`@[elementwise]`).
108	- Project-local `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality` (`StructureSheafModuleK.lean` L161) — the k-level version; the R-level analogue is what S7 needs.
109	
110	**Hard constraints**:
111	- **No new project-local helper lemmas** (user policy 2026-05-11). Use only Mathlib + the existing `algebraMap_naturality` family.
112	- **No new axioms.**
113	- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation.**
114	- Sorry-count budget: starts at 6; target **≤ 5** (close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`); hard cap **6** (no regression).

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
83	
84	**Format:**
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
88	
89	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
90	### Attempt 1
91	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
92	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
93	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
94	
95	### Attempt 2
96	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
97	- **Result:** RESOLVED
98	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
99	
100	## helper_bijective (line 78)
101	### Attempt 1
102	- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
103	- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
122	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
125	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
130	
131	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
132	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
133	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
134	   - Concrete next step for the next session
135	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
136	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)

6	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic

8	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.FinitePresentation
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth

16	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes
17	
18	This file constructs the sheaf of relative Kähler differentials `Ω_{X/S}`
19	of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S`, together with its universal derivation
20	`d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`. It also states the cotangent exact sequence and the
21	characterisation of smoothness in terms of local freeness of `Ω`.
22	
23	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
24	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
25	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
26	
27	## Status (iteration 064 — scaffold)
28	
29	All main declarations have `sorry` bodies. Closure trajectory is estimated
30	at ~10 iterations per `STRATEGY.md`.
31	
32	## References
33	
34	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
35	-/
36	
37	set_option autoImplicit false
38	
39	universe u
40	
41	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
42	
43	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
44	
45	variable {X Y S : Scheme.{u}}
46	
47	/-! ## The relative cotangent presheaf and sheaf -/
48	
49	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
50	
51	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
52	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
53	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
54	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
55	
56	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
57	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
58	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
59	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
60	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
61	    f.c
62	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
63	
64	/-! ### Sheaf condition: decomposition
65	
66	Closing `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` is the main outstanding
67	gap of Phase B step 1. The mathematical proof has three layers:
68	
69	1. **`KaehlerDifferential` commutes with localisation.** For a ring map
70	   `A → B` and `f ∈ B`, `Ω_{B[1/f]/A} ≅ Ω_{B/A} ⊗_B B[1/f]`. Mathlib's
71	   `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule` (and the scheme variant
72	   `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`) provides this.
73	2. **The presheaf is a sheaf on the basis of affine basic opens.** On a
74	   basic open `D(f)` of `Spec B`, sections are `Ω_{B[1/f]/A}`. Gluing on a
75	   basic-open cover `{D(fᵢ)}` of `Spec B` follows from the localisation
76	   compatibility of step 1, applied to each `fᵢ`.
77	3. **Globalisation to all opens.** A presheaf on a scheme is a sheaf iff
78	   it is a sheaf on the basis of affine opens (more precisely, on the
79	   basic opens of every affine open). This is the standard scheme
80	   sheaf-of-modules construction.
81	
82	The substep theorems below are the natural decomposition. Each remains
83	`sorry` pending the full proof; the structure mirrors the blueprint at
84	`blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`. -/
85	
86	/-- Substep 1 (localisation compatibility): For an affine open `V` of `X`
87	mapped to an affine open `U` of `S`, the sections of
88	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` over `V` are isomorphic (as additive groups)
89	to the Kähler differential module `KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app (op V))`. -/
90	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
91	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
92	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
93	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
94	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
95	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
96	  rfl
97	
98	/-- The presheaf of relative differentials is a **sheaf** in the Zariski
99	topology: Kähler differentials commute with localisation, which gives the
100	gluing axiom on affine opens.
101	
102	**Status (iter-065):** Sorry. Proof requires combining:
103	- Substep 1 (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler`, definitional)
104	- Substep 2: sheaf condition on the basis of basic opens, derived from
105	  `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule`
106	- Substep 3: globalisation from a basis to all opens (Mathlib has
107	  `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSheafUniqueGluing` and `isSheafFor` lemmas
108	  for restricting to a basis)
109	
110	The route via `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` is not viable since that
111	requires the result we are trying to prove (the presheaf must already be
112	a sheaf to be packaged as a `SheafOfModules`). -/
113	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf (f : X ⟶ S) :
114	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat)
115	      (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf := by
116	  -- Strategy: reduce to the underlying presheaf of types via
117	  -- `isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp`, then verify the sheaf condition on
118	  -- affine opens using `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`.
119	  -- The proof requires substantial development of a
120	  -- "sheaf condition restricted to affine basis" lemma + the localisation
121	  -- compatibility for Kähler differentials in the scheme-language.
122	  sorry
123	
124	/-- The sheaf of relative differentials `Ω_{X/S}`.
125	
126	Packages the presheaf `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` with the sheaf axiom
127	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` into the standard `X.Modules` shape. -/
128	noncomputable def relativeDifferentials (f : X ⟶ S) : X.Modules :=
129	  ⟨relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f, relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf f⟩
130	
131	/-! ## Universal derivation -/
132	
133	/-- The universal derivation `d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`.
134	
135	On each affine open `V = Spec B` over `Spec A`, this is the Mathlib universal
136	derivation `d_{B/A} : B → Ω_{B/A}`. It is an `A`-linear sheaf map satisfying
137	the Leibniz rule `d(ab) = a d(b) + b d(a)`. -/
138	noncomputable def universalDerivation (f : X ⟶ S) :
139	    X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat ⟶
140	      (relativeDifferentials f).val.presheaf := by
141	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
142	    f.c

699	/-- Cotangent exact sequence for a composition of schemes `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S`.
700	
701	For `f : X ⟶ Y` and `g : Y ⟶ S`, there is an exact sequence of
702	quasi-coherent `𝒪_X`-modules
703	```
704	  f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y} ⟶ 0.
705	```
706	Built on affine charts from the Mathlib ring-level cotangent exact
707	sequence (`KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`) and glued via the
708	compatibility above.
709	
710	The three sub-declarations `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`, `cotangentExactSeqBeta`,
711	and `cotangentExactSeq_structure` carry the remaining sorries; this theorem
712	itself is closed by assembling them.
713	
714	The composition-zero proof obligation is now an explicit existential
715	witness rather than an in-type `by sorry`, eliminating the iter-064/065
716	nested `by sorry` placeholder inside `ShortComplex.mk`. -/
717	theorem cotangent_exact_sequence (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
718	    ∃ (α : (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (relativeDifferentials g) ⟶
719	        relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g))
720	      (β : relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) ⟶ relativeDifferentials f)
721	      (h : α ≫ β = 0),
722	      CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.Exact
723	        (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk α β h) ∧
724	      CategoryTheory.Epi β := by
725	  obtain ⟨h, hex, hep⟩ := cotangentExactSeq_structure f g
726	  exact ⟨_, _, h, hex, hep⟩
727	
728	/-! ## Smoothness and local freeness of `Ω` -/
729	
730	/-- Smoothness of a finite-presentation morphism is equivalent to `Ω_{X/S}`
731	being locally free of the given rank.
732	
733	The forward direction is the Jacobian criterion; the converse follows from
734	the cotangent exact sequence and Nakayama's lemma applied at each point. -/
735	theorem smooth_iff_locally_free_omega (f : X ⟶ S)
736	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f) (n : ℕ) :
737	    Smooth f ↔
738	      ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : X.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
739	        let R := X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
740	        let M := (relativeDifferentials f).val.obj (.op U)
741	        Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
742	  sorry
743	
744	/-! ## Cotangent space at a section -/
745	
746	/-- If `f : X ⟶ S` is smooth and `s : S ⟶ X` is a section of `f`, then the
747	cotangent space `s^* Ω_{X/S}` is a locally free `𝒪_S`-module of rank `n`.
748	In particular, if `S = Spec k` and `X` is smooth of relative dimension `n`,
749	the cotangent space at the corresponding `k`-point is an `n`-dimensional
750	`k`-vector space. -/
751	theorem cotangent_at_section (f : X ⟶ S)
752	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f)
753	    (s : S ⟶ X) (hs : s ≫ f = 𝟙 S) (n : ℕ)
754	    (hsmooth : Smooth f) :
755	    ∀ (x : S), ∃ (U : S.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
756	      let R := S.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
757	      let M := ((Scheme.Modules.pullback s).obj (relativeDifferentials f)).val.obj (.op U)
758	      Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
759	  sorry
760	
761	/-! ## Restriction of scalars to k-modules -/
762	
763	/-- The `O_C(U)`-module structure on `M(U)` viewed as a `k`-module via
764	restriction of scalars along the algebra map `k → O_C(U)`. -/
765	noncomputable abbrev moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj
766	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
767	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
768	    (M : C.left.Modules)
769	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) : ModuleCat.{u} k :=
770	  (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom).obj (M.val.obj U)
771	
772	/-- Smul-naturality lemma for `moduleKPresheafOfModules`: the additive map
773	`M.val.presheaf.map f` commutes with the `k`-action on source and target after
774	restriction of scalars. -/
775	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_smul_compat
776	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
777	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
778	    (M : C.left.Modules)
779	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
780	    M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
781	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f := by
782	  -- Strategy: lift to ModuleCat.smul_naturality applied to (M.val.map f) over O_C, then transport
783	  -- the smul argument from `(kToSection C U).hom r` to `(kToSection C V).hom r` using algebraMap
784	  -- naturality.
785	  have hsmul_src : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r =
786	      (M.val.obj U).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) := rfl
787	  have hsmul_tgt : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
788	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := rfl
789	  rw [hsmul_src, hsmul_tgt]
790	  -- Now goal:
791	  --   M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (M.val.obj V).smul (kV r)
792	  --     = (M.val.obj U).smul (kU r) ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f
793	  have hpres : M.val.presheaf.map f =
794	      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat).map (M.val.map f) := by
795	    apply AddCommGrpCat.Hom.ext
796	    ext x
797	    exact PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe M.val f x
798	  rw [hpres]
799	  -- Now goal in terms of (forget₂).map (M.val.map f); use ModuleCat.smul_naturality:
800	  have hnat := ModuleCat.smul_naturality (M.val.map f) ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)
801	  -- hnat has smul on the *target* of (M.val.map f) at index (kU r).
802	  -- That target is (restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M.val.obj V), so its .smul (kU r)
803	  -- is definitionally (M.val.obj V).smul ((R.map f).hom (kU r)).
804	  -- By algebraMap_naturality, (R.map f).hom (kU r) = (kV r), which is what we need.
805	  have halg : (RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))
806	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) =
807	      (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r := by
808	    rw [← toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection, ← toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
809	    exact toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality (C := C) f r
810	  -- Convert hnat's target smul to the desired form via halg
811	  have htarget : ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars
812	        (RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))).obj (M.val.obj V)).smul
813	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) =
814	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := by
815	    change (M.val.obj V).smul ((RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))
816	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)) =
817	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r)
818	    rw [halg]
819	  rw [htarget] at hnat
820	  exact hnat
821	
822	/-- The restriction map for `moduleKPresheafOfModules`, extracted as a separate
823	definition so the elaborator does not have to inline the smul-naturality proof
824	inside the main `Functor` definition. -/
825	noncomputable def moduleKPresheafOfModules_map
826	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
827	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
828	    (M : C.left.Modules)
829	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
830	    moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U ⟶ moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V :=
831	  ModuleCat.homMk (M.val.presheaf.map f) (moduleKPresheafOfModules_smul_compat C M f)
832	
833	@[simp] lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂
834	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
835	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
836	    (M : C.left.Modules)
837	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
838	    (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map
839	        (moduleKPresheafOfModules_map C M f) = M.val.presheaf.map f := by
840	  unfold moduleKPresheafOfModules_map
841	  exact ModuleCat.forget₂_map_homMk _ _
842	
843	/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
844	scalars along the structure morphism `k → Γ(C, U)`.
845	
846	On each open `U`, the sections are the `O_C(U)`-module `M(U)` viewed as a
847	`k`-module via the algebra map `k → O_C(U)`. The restriction maps are `k`-linear
848	by the naturality of this algebra map. -/
849	noncomputable def moduleKPresheafOfModules
850	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
851	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
852	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
853	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
854	  obj U := moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U
855	  map f := moduleKPresheafOfModules_map C M f
856	  map_id U := by
857	    apply (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map_injective
858	    rw [moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
859	    exact M.val.presheaf.map_id U
860	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
861	    apply (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map_injective
862	    rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp]
863	    simp only [moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂]
864	    exact M.val.presheaf.map_comp f g
865	
866	/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained by restriction of scalars is a sheaf:
867	its underlying presheaf of abelian groups coincides with that of the original
868	`O_C`-module. -/
869	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf
870	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
871	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
872	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
873	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
874	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules C M) := by
875	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp _ _ (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
876	  convert M.isSheaf using 1
877	
878	/-- The sheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
879	scalars along the structure morphism. -/
880	noncomputable def moduleKSheafOfModules
881	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
882	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
883	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
884	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
885	  ⟨moduleKPresheafOfModules C M, moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf C M⟩
886	
887	/-! ## Serre duality genus equality -/
888	
889	/-- For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k`,
890	the dimension of `H^0(C, Ω_{C/k})` equals the dimension of `H^1(C, 𝒪_C)`,
891	and both equal the genus `g(C)`.
892	
893	This is the dimension-one case of Serre duality applied to the canonical
894	sheaf `ω_C = Ω_{C/k}`. -/
895	theorem serre_duality_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
896	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom]
897	    (hsmooth : Smooth C.hom) :
898	    Module.rank k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0) =
899	      Module.rank k
900	        (HModule k (moduleKSheafOfModules C (relativeDifferentials C.hom)) 0) := by
901	  sorry
902	
903	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
904

448	lemma cotangentExactSeq_structure (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
449	    ∃ (h : cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g ≫ cotangentExactSeqBeta f g = 0),

451	        (cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g) (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g) h).Exact ∧
452	      CategoryTheory.Epi (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g) := by
453	  -- Strategy preserved; closure is iter-078+. The h_zero ring-level input is
454	  -- `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`; h_exact has the same input
455	  -- + sheaf-level stalkwise bridge; h_epi uses `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective`
456	  -- + sheaf-level epi-from-presheaf bridge. The three sub-claims merge into one

463	
464	  -- Decomposition: produce the three named sub-claims in order. Each has a distinct
465	  -- ring-theoretic input via `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` / `map_surjective`.
466	  -- The composition-zero witness depends on the concrete definition of `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`,
467	  -- so closure here is blocked behind that definition's completion.
468	  -- Iter-073 closure attempt. The strategy is identified for all three sub-claims; the
469	  -- mechanical execution of the lower-level identities below is the next-iteration target.
470	  -- The skeleton documents the proof shape and the local Mathlib leverage names so the
471	  -- next prover can drop straight into the inner identity-chasing.
472	  --
473	  -- The universal property of `relativeDifferentials' φ` lets us prove equalities of
474	  -- morphisms out of `relativeDifferentials' φ` by checking they postcompose with the
475	  -- universal derivation `derivation' φ` to the same Derivation
476	  -- (`isUniversal'.postcomp_injective`). Composed with the adjunction injectivity for the
477	  -- pullback/pushforward pair, this reduces `α ≫ β = 0` to a pointwise statement about
478	  -- `derivation' φ2'`-values; the ring-level inputs are
479	  -- `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` (for `h_zero`/`h_exact`) and
480	  -- `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` (for `h_epi`).
481	  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
482	  · -- Sub-claim `h_zero` : `α ≫ β = 0`.
483	    --
484	    -- Strategy.
485	    -- Step 1: apply the adjunction injectivity for `Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction`.
486	    --   This converts the goal to a statement about the adjoint morphisms living in `Y.Modules`.
487	    -- Step 2: by `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right` and additivity of the adjunction
488	    --   hom-set bijection (`Adjunction.homAddEquiv_zero`), the goal becomes
489	    --     `(homEquiv α) ≫ (pushforward f).map β = 0`   in `Y.Modules`.
490	    -- Step 3: unfolding `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`, the LHS factor `homEquiv α` simplifies to
491	    --   `⟨(isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_target⟩` (via `Equiv.apply_symm_apply`) where `d_target`
492	    --   is the Derivation' valued in the pushed-forward `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g)`
493	    --   with `d_target.d b = D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b)` and `D_X := derivation' φ_fg'`.
494	    -- Step 4: drop to PresheafOfModules level via `SheafOfModules.hom_ext` (the morphisms
495	    --   agree iff their `.val`s agree).
496	    -- Step 5: apply `isUniversal'.postcomp_injective` to reduce the goal to a Derivation
497	    --   equality; both sides postcomposed with `derivation' φ_g'`.
498	    --   LHS-derivation maps `b ↦ (((pushforward f).map β.val).app U).hom (d_target.d b)
499	    --                            = (β.val.app (f⁻¹ U)).hom (D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b))`.
500	    --   RHS-derivation is zero (postcomp of zero is the zero derivation).
501	    -- Step 6: by `β.val`'s universal property (`(derivation' φ_fg').postcomp β.val = d1`,
502	    --   with `d1.d := d2.d := (derivation' φ2').d`), the LHS-derivation simplifies to
503	    --   `b ↦ d2.d ((f.c.app U).hom b)`.
504	    -- Step 7: the adjunction-coherence identity
505	    --   `f.c = adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward _).map φ2'`
506	    --   gives `(f.c.app U).hom b = (φ2'.app (f⁻¹ U)).hom ((adj_f.unit.app _).app U b)`,
507	    --   so by `derivation' φ2'.d_app` the result vanishes.
508	    --
509	    -- Mathlib leverage names (all confirmed in iter-072):
510	    --   * `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right` (Adjunction/Basic.lean L232)
511	    --   * `Adjunction.homAddEquiv_zero` (Adjunction/Additive.lean L66)
512	    --   * `Equiv.apply_symm_apply`
513	    --   * `SheafOfModules.hom_ext` (ModuleCat/Sheaf.lean L53)
514	    --   * `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal'.postcomp_injective`
515	    --     (ModuleCat/Differentials/Presheaf.lean L101)
516	    --   * `PresheafOfModules.Derivation.d_app` (ModuleCat/Differentials/Presheaf.lean L63)
517	    --
518	    -- Tactic chain (Steps 1-7 of the strategy):
519	    apply ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).injective
520	    rw [Adjunction.homAddEquiv_zero, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
521	    -- Goal: `(homEquiv α) ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map β = 0` in `Y.Modules`.
522	    -- Unfold α to expose `homEquiv α = ⟨isUniversal'.desc d_target⟩`:
523	    unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha
524	    simp only [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
525	    -- Goal: `⟨(isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_target⟩ ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map β = 0`
526	    --   in `Y.Modules`.
527	    -- Step 4: drop to PresheafOfModules level via `SheafOfModules.hom_ext`, then unfold
528	    -- the `.val` of the pushforward of `β` to expose
529	    -- `(PresheafOfModules.pushforward _).map β.val`.
530	    apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
531	    simp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]
532	    -- Re-introduce local names for the ring presheaf morphisms inlined from
533	    -- `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`, so that the universal-property handle elaborates.
534	    set φ_g' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf ⟶ Y.presheaf :=
535	      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
536	        S.presheaf Y.presheaf).symm g.c with hφ_g'
537	    set φ_fg' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).obj S.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf :=
538	      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
539	        S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c with hφ_fg'
540	    set φ_2' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf :=
541	      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv
542	        Y.presheaf X.presheaf).symm f.c with hφ_2'
543	    set adj_f := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base with hadj_f
544	    -- Step 5: apply `isUniversal'.postcomp_injective`. Two morphisms out of

548	    -- Goal: equality of two `((pushforward).obj (relativeDifferentials' φ_fg'))`-valued
549	    -- Derivations over `φ_g'`. The RHS is the zero Derivation (postcomp of the zero hom).

553	    --
554	    -- LHS `.d` at `b : Y.presheaf.obj U.unop` reduces, via the postcomp definition and the
555	    -- universal-property fact `(derivation' φ_g').postcomp αv = d_target`, to
556	    --   (β.val.app (f⁻¹ U) .hom) (D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b))
557	    -- where `D_X := derivation' φ_fg'`. By `β`'s universal property
558	    --   `(derivation' φ_fg').postcomp β.val = d1`   with `d1.d := (derivation' φ_2').d`
559	    -- (the `fac` from inside `cotangentExactSeqBeta`), this equals
560	    --   (derivation' φ_2').d ((f.c.app U).hom b).
561	    -- By the adjunction-coherence identity
562	    --   `f.c = adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward _).map φ_2'`   (rfl)
563	    -- we have `(f.c.app U).hom b = (φ_2'.app (f⁻¹ U) .hom) ((adj_f.unit.app _).app U b)`.
564	    -- Then `Derivation'.d_app` for `φ_2'` (the universal derivation vanishes on the source
565	    -- presheaf of `φ_2'`) gives zero.
566	    --
567	    -- Note. `d_target` and `D_X` from `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`'s body are not in scope
568	    -- here (they are internal `let`s); they appear inline in the goal post-`unfold`.
569	    -- We work abstractly: build coherence and `d_app` ahead, then let simp + Universal.fac
570	    -- collapse the chain.

572	    -- (a) Adjunction-coherence: f.c = adj_f.unit ≫ pushforward.map φ_2' (rfl chain).
573	    have hcoh : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫
574	        (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_2' = f.c := by
575	      have h1 : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫
576	          (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_2' =
577	        adj_f.homEquiv Y.presheaf X.presheaf φ_2' := rfl
578	      rw [h1, hφ_2']
579	      exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
580	    -- (c) Pointwise coherence at (U, b).

582	        (φ_2'.app (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))).hom
583	          ((adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf).app U b) := by
584	      have h1 := congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom (NatTrans.app h U) b) hcoh.symm
585	      simpa using h1
586	    -- (d) d_app for derivation' φ_2' via coherence.
587	    have hd_app : (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_2').d
588	        (X := Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))
589	        ((f.c.app U).hom b) = 0 := by
590	      rw [hcoh_app]
591	      exact PresheafOfModules.Derivation'.d_app _ _
592	    -- (e) Reduce postcomp on both sides, split the composition αv ≫ βv, identify αv's
593	    --     effect via the universal property of `derivation' φ_g'`, then collapse via β.val's

599	    -- description of `(pushforward _).map β.val` at U).

601	               PresheafOfModules.comp_app, PresheafOfModules.pushforward_map_app_apply,
602	               Limits.zero_app, ModuleCat.hom_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply, ModuleCat.hom_comp,

614	    -- We close the goal by chaining two pointwise instances of `Universal.fac` (one

lemma cotangentExactSeq_structure (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
    ∃ (h : cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g ≫ cotangentExactSeqBeta f g = 0),
      (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk
        (cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g) (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g) h).Exact ∧
      CategoryTheory.Epi (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g) := by
  -- Inline iter-076 chain (h_zero), preserved verbatim from the disabled block.
  -- Steps 1–7 of the strategy reduce `α ≫ β = 0` to the pointwise statement
  -- `(derivation' φ_2').d ((f.c.app U).hom b) = 0`, which follows from the
  -- adjunction coherence `hcoh` together with `Derivation'.d_app`.
  have h_zero : cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g ≫ cotangentExactSeqBeta f g = 0 := by
    apply ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).injective
    rw [Adjunction.homAddEquiv_zero, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
    unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha
    simp only [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
    apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
    simp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]
    set φ_g' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf ⟶ Y.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
        S.presheaf Y.presheaf).symm g.c with hφ_g'
    set φ_fg' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).obj S.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
        S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c with hφ_fg'
    set φ_2' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv
        Y.presheaf X.presheaf).symm f.c with hφ_2'
    set adj_f := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base with hadj_f
    apply (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').postcomp_injective
    ext U b
    have hcoh : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫
        (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_2' = f.c := by
      have h1 : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫
          (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_2' =
          adj_f.homEquiv Y.presheaf X.presheaf φ_2' := rfl
      rw [h1, hφ_2']
      exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
    have hcoh_app : (f.c.app U).hom b =
        (φ_2'.app (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))).hom
          ((adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf).app U b) := by
      have h1 := congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom (NatTrans.app h U) b) hcoh.symm
      simpa using h1
    have hd_app : (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_2').d
        (X := Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))
        ((f.c.app U).hom b) = 0 := by
      rw [hcoh_app]
      exact PresheafOfModules.Derivation'.d_app _ _
    simp only [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply,
               PresheafOfModules.comp_app, PresheafOfModules.pushforward_map_app_apply,
               Limits.zero_app, ModuleCat.hom_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply, ModuleCat.hom_comp,
               LinearMap.comp_apply]
    have hα_fac : ∀ (d_t : ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
          (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))).Derivation' φ_g')
        (b' : Y.presheaf.obj U),
        (((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_t).app U).hom
          ((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_g').d b') = d_t.d b' := by
      intro d_t b'
      have hfac := (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').fac d_t
      have hpt := PresheafOfModules.Derivation.congr_d hfac b'
      simpa [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply] using hpt
    rw [hα_fac _ b]
    dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
    unfold cotangentExactSeqBeta
    have hβ_fac : ∀ (d_t : (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'
          φ_2').Derivation' φ_fg')
        (b' : X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))),
        (((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_fg').desc d_t).app
            (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))).hom
          ((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_fg').d b') = d_t.d b' := by
      intro d_t b'
      have hfac := (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_fg').fac d_t
      have hpt := PresheafOfModules.Derivation.congr_d hfac b'
      simpa [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply] using hpt
    rw [hβ_fac _ ((f.c.app U).hom b)]
    exact hd_app
  refine ⟨h_zero, ?_⟩
  -- h_exact ∧ h_epi: bundled. The `epi_of_epi_presheaf` gap-fill above is

lemma cotangentExactSeq_structure (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
    ∃ (h : cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g ≫ cotangentExactSeqBeta f g = 0),
      (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk
        (cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g) (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g) h).Exact ∧
      CategoryTheory.Epi (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g) := by
  -- Inline iter-076 chain (h_zero), preserved verbatim from the disabled block.
  -- Steps 1–7 of the strategy reduce `α ≫ β = 0` to the pointwise statement
  -- `(derivation' φ_2').d ((f.c.app U).hom b) = 0`, which follows from the
  -- adjunction coherence `hcoh` together with `Derivation'.d_app`.
  have h_zero : cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g ≫ cotangentExactSeqBeta f g = 0 := by
    apply ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).injective
    rw [Adjunction.homAddEquiv_zero, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
    unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha
    simp only [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
    apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
    simp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]
    set φ_g' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf ⟶ Y.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
        S.presheaf Y.presheaf).symm g.c with hφ_g'
    set φ_fg' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).obj S.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
        S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c with hφ_fg'
    set φ_2' : (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf :=
      ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv
        Y.presheaf X.presheaf).symm f.c with hφ_2'
    set adj_f := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base with hadj_f
    apply (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').postcomp_injective
    ext U b
    have hcoh : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫
        (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_2' = f.c := by
      have h1 : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫
          (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_2' =
          adj_f.homEquiv Y.presheaf X.presheaf φ_2' := rfl
      rw [h1, hφ_2']
      exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
    have hcoh_app : (f.c.app U).hom b =
        (φ_2'.app (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))).hom
          ((adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf).app U b) := by
      have h1 := congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom (NatTrans.app h U) b) hcoh.symm
      simpa using h1
    have hd_app : (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_2').d
        (X := Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))
        ((f.c.app U).hom b) = 0 := by
      rw [hcoh_app]
      exact PresheafOfModules.Derivation'.d_app _ _
    simp only [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply,
               PresheafOfModules.comp_app, PresheafOfModules.pushforward_map_app_apply,
               Limits.zero_app, ModuleCat.hom_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply, ModuleCat.hom_comp,
               LinearMap.comp_apply]
    have hα_fac : ∀ (d_t : ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
          (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))).Derivation' φ_g')
        (b' : Y.presheaf.obj U),
        (((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_t).app U).hom
          ((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_g').d b') = d_t.d b' := by
      intro d_t b'
      have hfac := (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').fac d_t
      have hpt := PresheafOfModules.Derivation.congr_d hfac b'
      simpa [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply] using hpt
    rw [hα_fac _ b]
    dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
    unfold cotangentExactSeqBeta
    have hβ_fac : ∀ (d_t : (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'
          φ_2').Derivation' φ_fg')
        (b' : X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))),
        (((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_fg').desc d_t).app
            (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))).hom
          ((PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_fg').d b') = d_t.d b' := by
      intro d_t b'
      have hfac := (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_fg').fac d_t
      have hpt := PresheafOfModules.Derivation.congr_d hfac b'
      simpa [PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply] using hpt
    rw [hβ_fac _ ((f.c.app U).hom b)]
    exact hd_app
  refine ⟨h_zero, ?_⟩
  -- h_exact ∧ h_epi: bundled. The `epi_of_epi_presheaf` gap-fill above is
  -- in place; the surjectivity of `(cotangentExactSeqBeta f g).val.app U`
  -- and a `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` route for the exactness are
  -- the residual work — see task_results for the current closure plan.
  sorry

  -- bridge that the `h_epi` half consumes.
  sorry

# AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean — iter-079

**Lane 1 goal:** Close `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (L460), reducing the file
sorry count from 5 → 4.

**Result:** PARTIAL — gap-fill helper `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf`
landed (no sorry); the central `cotangentExactSeq_structure` body remains a
single `sorry` due to an iter-078 elaboration shift that breaks the iter-076
preserved chain.  File compiles cleanly with **5 sorries** (no regression);
the cap of 5 holds.  The new gap-fill is the SheafOfModules→PresheafOfModules
epi-reflection bridge that `h_epi` will consume in iter-080.

## Concrete changes

1. **Added `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf`** (L437–443).  One-step bridge
   using `Functor.epi_of_epi_map` plus
   `SheafOfModules.instFaithfulPresheafOfModulesValRingCatForget`:
   ```lean
   lemma _root_.SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf
       {C : Type*} [Category C] {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
       {R : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} {F G : SheafOfModules R} (f : F ⟶ G)
       (h : CategoryTheory.Epi f.val) : CategoryTheory.Epi f := by
     have : CategoryTheory.Epi ((SheafOfModules.forget R).map f) := by
       rw [SheafOfModules.forget_map]; exact h
     exact (SheafOfModules.forget R).epi_of_epi_map this
   ```
   Flagged with `-- GAP-FILL (iter-079, Lane 1):` comment.  This is one of
   the two gap-fills explicitly permitted by the iter-079 plan.

2. **Updated `cotangentExactSeq_structure` docstring** (L445–459) to record the
   iter-079 closure status:
   > Status (iter-079): `h_zero` closure scaffolded inline using the iter-076
   > preserved chain (universal-property-based reduction to `derivation' φ_2'`-
   > vanishing via adjunction coherence).  `h_exact` and `h_epi` remain bundled
   > inside a single trailing `sorry` for the conjunction `Exact ∧ Epi`,
   > pending the semantic gap-fills `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` (not
   > yet introduced) and an effective surjectivity proof for
   > `(cotangentExactSeqBeta f g).val.app U` (the `epi_of_epi_presheaf` bridge
   > above is in place; the surjectivity itself is the remaining work for the
   > `h_epi` half).

3. **Inserted detailed diagnostic comments in the body** of
   `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (L465–477) documenting precisely what fails
   in the iter-076 chain when applied to the iter-078 shape.  See attempts
   below.

## Attempt log

### `cotangentExactSeq_structure (L460)` — bundle of (`h_zero`, `h_exact`, `h_epi`)

#### Attempt 1 — Inline iter-076 preserved chain (full transcription)

- **Approach:** Extract `h_zero` via `have`, then `refine ⟨h_zero, ?_⟩; sorry`.
  The chain begins with:
  ```
  apply ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).injective
  rw [Adjunction.homAddEquiv_zero, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
  unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha
  simp only [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
  simp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]
  -- (...)
  ```
- **Result:** FAILED at the second `simp only` call.  Error: `` `simp` made no
  progress``.
- **Root causes (both confirmed via `lean_multi_attempt`):**
  1. The iter-078 composition `(... ≫ (Modules.pushforward f).map β).val` is
     reduced eagerly by the elaborator to a raw structure form (verified by
     `dsimp only [CategoryTheory.CategoryStruct.comp, SheafOfModules.instCategory]`
     unfolding to `{ app := ..., naturality := ... }.val.app x = ...`), so
     `(f ≫ g).val` no longer occurs as a redex for `SheafOfModules.comp_val`.
     A direct `rw [SheafOfModules.comp_val]` after `apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext`
     errors with "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern `(?f ≫ ?g).val`."
  2. The Mathlib lemma `SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val` (in
     `Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean`) is about
     the **continuous-functor** variant `SheafOfModules.pushforward`, not the
     project-level `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforward`.  Looking up
     `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` confirms it is its own definition with its
     own `Hom.app` / `pushforward_map_app` API (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.
     Modules.Sheaf`) rather than reusing `SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val`.
     The chain's simp lemma name is therefore stale for the iter-078 shape.
- **Dead end:** Do NOT use `simp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val,
  SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]` after `apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext` in
  iter-078 onward.

#### Attempt 2 — Alternative reduction tactics tried via `lean_multi_attempt`

- `simp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val]` alone — FAILED (same "made no progress").
- `rw [SheafOfModules.comp_val]` — FAILED (pattern not found, as above).
- `rfl` after `apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext` — FAILED (LHS/RHS not defeq).
- `change _ = (0 : _ ⟶ _).val` — FAILED (field-projection error: `0` infers
  to `ℕ` without type-class hints).
- `dsimp only [CategoryTheory.CategoryStruct.comp, SheafOfModules.instCategory]`
  — succeeded (no error) but unfolded the composition to the explicit
  `{ app := ..., naturality := ... }.val.app x` form, which is *not* a redex
  for the iter-076 simp chain.
- `apply PresheafOfModules.Hom.ext` (after `hom_ext`) — FAILED to unify;
  the goal at that point is not yet in `f.app = g.app` form.

#### Status

- Body of `cotangentExactSeq_structure` left as `sorry` with inline
  documentation describing exactly the iter-080 unblock step (see body).
- The disabled-chain comment block (L480–717) is preserved verbatim so the
  next iteration can reuse the strategy with the iter-080 simp adjustment.

## Concrete next step for iter-080

The unblock requires replacing the failing
`simp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]` with
the iter-078-compatible analogue.  Two routes:

1. **Manual `change` route.**  After `apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext`, the goal
   has shape `(X ≫ Y).val = 0.val` but with the LHS displayed as
   `({val := A} ≫ B).val`.  A targeted `change A ≫ B.val = 0` (with the
   correct PresheafOfModules-level term for the LHS, plus an explicit
   description of `B.val = (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).map
   (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g).val` from the project-local
   `Scheme.Modules.pushforward_map_val_eq` if such a lemma is introduced)
   should put the goal in the form the iter-076 chain expects.

2. **Per-section route via `Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app`.**  Use
   `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.Hom.ext` (per-section extensionality) and
   `Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app` / `Scheme.Modules.pushforward_map_app` to
   reduce to a per-`U` PresheafOfModules.Hom equality directly, bypassing the
   `.val` step entirely.  This is the iter-078-native shape.

The `epi_of_epi_presheaf` gap-fill (added this iteration) is the bridge that
`h_epi` requires once we route to PresheafOfModules-level epi via
`PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective` plus the
`KaehlerDifferential.span_range_derivation` argument for the descended
`cotangentExactSeqBeta f g .val.app U` surjectivity.

For `h_exact`, the previously-permitted gap-fill `SheafOfModules.
exact_iff_stalkwise` (analogue of `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` for stalks) was
NOT introduced this iteration — adding it with a `sorry` body would push the
file to 6 sorries (over the iter-079 hard cap), and proving it from scratch
requires the `TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor`-preserves-exactness chain that is
multiple iterations of infrastructure.  Iter-080 should re-evaluate whether
this gap-fill is the right route, or whether `h_exact` should be split into
its own dispatched lane with a higher LOC budget.
